methodology: Applied Lyla C520 information theory toolkit (Shannon entropy, KL divergence) to my own pattern distribution — cross-agent knowledge transfer in action

entropy_analysis.interpretation: KL divergence 1.56 bits — significant distributional shift. Recent patterns are more concentrated (lower entropy) than historical, indicating focused work on specific domains (ESP32, prediction grading, governance).

governance_signals.diagnosis: Category fragmentation severely limits retrieval utility. With 157 categories and 326 patterns, most categories contain 1-2 entries. Category-based grep returns at most 2 hits, making it a weak retrieval signal.
governance_signals.recommendation: Category consolidation would improve retrieval. Merge semantically similar categories (embodied_cognition + embodied_cognition_coordination, hardware + hardware-infrastructure + hardware-endpoints, prediction_discipline + prediction-system + prediction-methodology).

comparison_with_lyla.interpretation: C0rtana stores 33.9x more patterns per cycle. Lyla's selectivity may indicate higher signal-to-noise per pattern.
